Scenario.title: Ransomware Attack on Water/Wastewater Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex A sophisticated ransomware campaign targeting critical water/wastewater infrastructure leverages AI-driven reconnaissance, compromised cloud resources, and legacy system vulnerabilities to exfiltrate data and disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Target Artifact (Tasks), File System, Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Windows Registry, Credential Store, Cloud Instance, Network Traffic, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Malicious Shell Modification, Delivery Method, Application Log Content, Windows Registry Key Creation, Pass the Hash, Process Injection, Process Creation, Reg.exe Utility, Network Traffic Flow, USB Memory, DNS Queries, Disabling Security Tools

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker deploys AI algorithms to analyze publicly available documents and operational reports, identifying vulnerable SCADA systems and employee contact information for targeted phishing campaigns.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The group modifies legitimate administrative scripts in cloud storage environments to embed ransomware payloads, preparing them for deployment during initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2013-2551 (Suggested): Use-after-free vulnerability in Microsoft Internet Explorer
stage.description: Phishing emails with malicious attachments are sent to facility managers, exploiting outdated browsers to deliver payloads that compromise endpoint devices.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-1350 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows DNS Server vulnerability
stage.description: Exploiting unpatched DNS servers, the attacker executes arbitrary code to deploy ransomware on industrial control systems managing water treatment processes.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A persistent scheduled task is created in the registry to automatically re-run the ransomware payload upon system reboot, ensuring long-term access.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery
stage.description: Stolen credentials from initial access are used to escalate privileges, granting administrative rights to critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate system processes to evade detection by security software and hide command-and-control communications.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Compromised deployment tools extract credentials stored in cloud-based configuration repositories, expanding the attacker's access across the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network traffic is monitored using Reg.exe to map internal network segments and identify high-value targets like supervisory control systems.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Default admin credentials are used to move laterally across the network, compromising additional systems and spreading the ransomware payload.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Sensitive data is copied to removable storage devices and physically removed from the facility, bypassing network monitoring mechanisms.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is encrypted and exfiltrated via DNS tunneling, disguising malicious traffic as legitimate domain queries to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Security software is disabled by modifying registry entries, allowing the ransomware to encrypt critical water treatment system files and disrupt operations.

Scenario.summary: This multi-stage attack compromises water/wastewater infrastructure through phishing, exploiting legacy systems, and deploying ransomware to disrupt operations while evading detection. The campaign highlights the risks of unpatched software, poor credential management, and inadequate network segmentation in critical sectors.
